name: react-luau-props-helper
description: Customizable prop autocomplete for React-Luau createElement calls.

extends.description: Name of the class to inherit props from (e.g. "Frame").

props.description: Additional props specific to this component.

reactLuauPropsHelper.createElementAliase.markdownDescription: Function names treated as React-Luau `createElement` calls. Each entry must be a Lua identifier (dots allowed). Add your own aliases here, e.g. `"r"` if you use `local r = React.createElement`.

enumDescriptions: Insert `Name = ` and place the cursor after the equals sign.
Insert `Name = ,` with the first tab stop at the value and the final tab stop after the comma (recommended for Lua table syntax).
reactLuauPropsHelper.snippetMode.markdownDescription: Controls what gets inserted when you accept a completion.

reactLuauPropsHelper.typeAwareValues.markdownDescription: When the prop's type is known (e.g. `BackgroundColor3` is `Color3`), insert a value template alongside the prop name. For example, `BackgroundColor3` becomes `BackgroundColor3 = Color3.fromRGB(255, 255, 255)` with tab stops at each colour channel. Disable for the older minimal-snippet behavior.

reactLuauPropsHelper.documentSymbols.ena.markdownDescription: When enabled (default), the extension provides Document Symbols for the React tree — visible in the Outline panel, the Breadcrumbs bar, and `Cmd+Shift+O` (Go to Symbol in File). Disable if you prefer your editor's outline to show only top-level Lua structure.

reactLuauPropsHelper.colorPreview.enable.markdownDescription: When enabled (default), `Color3.fromRGB(...)` and `Color3.new(...)` get a gutter swatch and a colour picker. Disable if another extension (such as a Roblox API extension) already provides this and you're seeing duplicate pickers.

reactLuauPropsHelper.inlayHints.enabled.markdownDescription: When enabled (default), every multi-line `e(...)` call gets a label at its closing `)` showing the element's class. VS Code's master toggle is `editor.inlayHints.enabled`; this lets you disable just this extension's hints without affecting others.

enumDescriptions: Show hints for every multi-line createElement call in the file.
Only show hints for calls that contain the cursor (the innermost call and each of its ancestors). Keeps the rest of the file uncluttered. (Default.)
reactLuauPropsHelper.inlayHints.scope.markdownDescription: How many inlay hints to show at once.

enumDescriptions: Render the label between the closing `)` and the trailing `,`.
Render the label after the trailing `,`, so the comma stays glued to the closing `)`. (Default.)
reactLuauPropsHelper.inlayHints.position.markdownDescription: Where to render the inlay-hint label relative to the trailing comma.

reactLuauPropsHelper.warnReservedPropNam.markdownDescription: Warn when a custom component declares a `---@prop` whose name shadows a Roblox property of its declared (or detected) base class. Off by default to avoid surprising users with new warnings.

reactLuauPropsHelper.deprecationDiagnost.markdownDescription: Show diagnostics for deprecated patterns: `Font = Enum.Font.X` (use `FontFace`), `TextColor = ...` (typo of `TextColor3`). Quick-fixes available via the lightbulb.

reactLuauPropsHelper.autoImport.enabled.markdownDescription: Opt-in. When enabled, `e(SomeComponent, …)` for a component the workspace knows about but the current file doesn't `require` produces an Information diagnostic with a quick-fix that inserts the require line.

enumDescriptions: Compute a `script.Parent…X` chain from the current file's directory to the component's file.
Use one of the configured aliases below. Falls back to `relative` if no alias matches.
reactLuauPropsHelper.autoImport.style.markdownDescription: How to build the `require(...)` path for auto-imports.

filesystemPath.description: Workspace-relative path prefix on disk, e.g. `src/Client/UI/Components`.

robloxPath.description: Roblox-style prefix to substitute, e.g. `script.Components` or `ReplicatedStorage.Packages`.

reactLuauPropsHelper.autoImport.aliases.markdownDescription: For `alias` style: map workspace-relative filesystem prefixes to Roblox-style require prefixes. The first matching alias wins.

scripts.pretest: npm run compile && npm run lint
